summary: Removed `After=default.target` from `/home/papa/.config/systemd/user/llama-swap.service`. This line created a circular ordering dependency: default.target wants both llama-swap and llama-swap-proxy (via default.target.wants/ symlinks); llama-swap-proxy has After=llama-swap.service; llama-swap had After=default.target — completing the cycle. Systemd was randomly breaking the cycle at each boot by dropping one job, causing inconsistent startup failures. The fix removes the bogus After= (a service is never ordered after the target it helps constitute). Both services now start cleanly with no cycle warnings. Linger=yes is already set on the user so the user session starts at boot.
details: File changed: /home/papa/.config/systemd/user/llama-swap.service — removed `After=default.target` line (5 lines → 4 lines in [Unit] section). No new dependencies introduced. Security: no implications. The `WantedBy=default.target` install directive is correct and untouched — it is what pulls the service into the boot sequence. Only the spurious `After=` ordering constraint was removed.
